hook.headline: Tailwind v4 + shadcn registry = shippable design systems in 5 lines.

cards.label: 01 · tailwind
cards.title: Tailwind v4 is native CSS now.
cards.body: v4 dropped the JS config — everything is CSS variables, processed at build time. Smaller runtime, faster Vite/Next builds, easier to theme. If you're still on v3 you're already behind.
cards.metric: → 5× faster builds · zero JS at runtime

cards.label: 02 · registry
cards.title: shadcn registry = npm for components.
cards.body: Publish your own component registry (or use a team's), then anyone runs `npx shadcn add @yourcompany/button` and gets your version of Button — code dropped into their repo, not a dep.

cards.label: 03 · why
cards.title: Bring-your-own design system.
cards.body: Old way: build a component library, publish to npm, version-lock all consumers. New way: publish a registry, consumers copy the code, own it, modify it. No version hell.

cards.label: 04 · setup
cards.title: Set up your own in 10 minutes.
cards.body: Create a registry.json with your component manifests, host it on Vercel (or any static), point shadcn CLI at it. Done. Your whole team can `shadcn add` from your URL.

cards.label: 05 · examples
cards.title: Three registries worth stealing from.
cards.body: 1) Tremor (charts). 2) Aceternity (animated heroes). 3) Magic UI (gradients + glows). All shadcn-compatible. Mix and match — they all use the same Button under the hood.

take.use_if: Your team rebuilds the same 5 components in every project — package them as a registry once.
take.skip_if: You're a solo dev with 1 active project — copy-paste from shadcn docs is still faster than maintaining a registry.
